fed_stance.label: Neutral hold
fed_stance.source: CME FedWatch continued to show the next Fed meeting overwhelmingly priced as no change, so the Fed input remains a neutral hold.

real_yields.label: Flat
real_yields.source: FRED DFII10 was previously verified at 1.95 on 2026-04-09, and no fresh >5 bp directional move was directly confirmed in this run, so real yields remain flat.

usd_dxy.label: Weak/falling
usd_dxy.source: TradingView DXY was previously captured at 98.697, down 0.10% on the day and down 1.48% on the week, which keeps the dollar factor in weak/falling territory.

risk_mood.label: Balanced
risk_mood.source: The Cboe VIX remained in the 15-20 band during the browsing session, so the regime is balanced rather than fully risk-on or risk-off.

vix_direction.label: Falling
vix_direction.source: The Cboe VIX daily change was previously verified as negative on the session, so volatility direction remains a supportive falling signal.

growth_narrative.label: Slowing
growth_narrative.source: Atlanta Fed GDPNow stood at 1.3% on 2026-04-09 versus 1.6% previously, so the U.S. growth narrative remains slowing.

oil_supply_shock.label: Tightening
oil_supply_shock.source: The latest accessible energy balance context still reflected tightening supply conditions, with no verified evidence in this run that the prior tightening classification had reversed.

oil_inventories.label: Neutral
oil_inventories.source: No clean fresh inventory build/draw figure was directly exposed in this browsing run, so the inventory factor is kept neutral rather than guessed.

gold_etf_flows.label: Supportive proxy
gold_etf_flows.source: Accessible World Gold Council market commentary remained supportive of gold demand and ETF-flow conditions, so the gold-flow proxy stays positive.

credit_spreads.label: Stable
credit_spreads.source: Because a fresh narrowing or widening move was not cleanly re-established from the latest FRED high-yield OAS page during this run, credit spreads are treated conservatively as stable.

sox.label: Rising
sox.source: TradingView SOX was previously verified at 8,889.83, up 2.31% on the day and 17.83% on the week, preserving a rising semiconductor signal.

move_index.label: Falling
move_index.source: TradingView MOVE was previously verified at 72.1541, down 2.51% on the day, so the Treasury-volatility factor remains supportive.

yield_curve_2s10s.label: Flat
yield_curve_2s10s.source: FRED T10Y2Y printed 0.52 on 2026-04-13. Without a sufficiently large confirmed directional change versus the recent baseline, the curve factor is treated as flat.

copper.label: Falling
copper.source: An accessible TradingEconomics copper page dated 2026-04-13 stated that copper futures fell more than 1% to below $5.8/lb, so copper is classified as falling.

ecb_stance.label: Mildly hawkish hold
ecb_stance.source: The ECB on 2026-03-19 kept rates unchanged but emphasized upside inflation risks tied to energy and the Middle East conflict, which supports a mildly hawkish hold classification.

rate_diff_eur_usd.label: Stable
rate_diff_eur_usd.source: With the Fed neutral and the ECB on hold rather than actively easing, the near-term EUR-USD policy differential is best treated as stable.

eurozone_growth.label: Neutral/unclear
eurozone_growth.source: A directly readable official euro-area release was not accessible in this run, and blocked secondary sources prevented full verification, so eurozone growth is kept neutral.

rba_stance.label: Hawkish/tightening
rba_stance.source: The RBA decision dated 2026-03-17 raised the cash rate target by 25 bps to 4.10%, which is a hawkish tightening signal.

rate_diff_aud_usd.label: Widening vs USD
rate_diff_aud_usd.source: The March RBA hike against a neutral Fed still leaves the short-rate differential more supportive for AUD than for USD.

risk_sentiment_aud.label: Neutral
risk_sentiment_aud.source: The broad volatility regime is balanced rather than decisively risk-on or risk-off, so the AUD-specific sentiment factor is neutral.

china_growth.label: Accelerating
china_growth.source: The official NBS release for March 2026 showed manufacturing PMI at 50.4, up from 49.0 and back above the 50 threshold.

boj_stance.label: Hold
boj_stance.source: The Bank of Japan statement dated 2026-03-19 kept the uncollateralized overnight call rate at around 0.75%, so the immediate meeting signal is a hold.

rate_diff_jpy_usd.label: Improving for JPY
rate_diff_jpy_usd.source: A neutral Fed combined with the post-normalization BoJ backdrop keeps the policy-divergence story favorable for yen futures.

geopolitical_risk.label: Stable
geopolitical_risk.source: No fresh directly verified escalation metric was collected in this run beyond the existing background risk, so geopolitical risk is kept stable.
